observed_mechanics.name: Unit Placement
observed_mechanics.description: Player drags a unit character from a bottom UI roster and drops it onto an empty floor/slot within their castle.

observed_mechanics.name: Trajectory Aiming and Firing
observed_mechanics.description: Player taps and drags on a placed unit to pull back a dotted trajectory line, then releases to fire a projectile at the enemy castle.

observed_mechanics.name: Destructible Environment / Health
observed_mechanics.description: Projectiles hit the enemy castle, causing explosions, reducing a top health bar percentage, and visually destroying chunks of the castle.

ui_elements_observed: Top health bars with percentages (Blue vs Red)

playable_candidates.concept_one_liner: Focus purely on the satisfying artillery aiming and destruction mechanic.
playable_candidates.hook_first_3_seconds: A pulsing animated hand prompts the player to drag back on a pre-placed skeleton unit, revealing a dotted trajectory line aimed at the enemy castle.

core_interaction.what_player_does: Drag back from the unit to aim the trajectory, then release to fire a rocket at the enemy castle.
core_interaction.win_state: Hitting the enemy castle 2 times reduces its health to 0% and triggers a massive explosion.

end_card.headline: Destroy the Enemy!
end_card.visual_treatment: The enemy castle crumbles into rubble, confetti explodes, and the surviving units cheer as the CTA button pulses.

predicted_appeal.rationale: Slingshot mechanics are universally understood, highly tactile, and immediately satisfying, making for a perfect high-converting playable.

playable_candidates.implementation_notes_for_codegen: Use a simple quadratic bezier curve for the trajectory line. The physics don't need to be perfectly realistic, just visually satisfying with a clear arc and explosion on impact.

playable_candidates.concept_one_liner: Combine the strategic unit placement with an automated satisfying payoff.
playable_candidates.hook_first_3_seconds: The player's castle is empty. A glowing arrow points from a goblin in the bottom UI to an empty slot in the castle.

core_interaction.what_player_does: Drag two units from the bottom roster into the castle slots.
core_interaction.win_state: Once placed, the units automatically rapid-fire at the enemy castle, destroying it in a spectacular automated sequence.

end_card.headline: Build Your Army!
end_card.visual_treatment: Screen transitions to a lineup of cool unlockable units with the CTA button below.

predicted_appeal.rationale: Appeals to strategy and merge-game players who enjoy building a setup and watching it execute, though less tactile than aiming.

playable_candidates.implementation_notes_for_codegen: This avoids complex physics. Simply use CSS/canvas animations to move projectiles horizontally with a slight arc once the drop event is registered.

playable_candidates.concept_one_liner: A full micro-loop: place a unit, then aim it to save your dying castle.
playable_candidates.hook_first_3_seconds: The player's castle is taking damage. A prompt urges them to drag a unit to the last remaining slot to counter-attack.

core_interaction.what_player_does: Drag a unit to the castle, then immediately drag on that unit to aim and fire a massive finishing blow.
core_interaction.win_state: The fired projectile hits the enemy castle, destroying it instantly.
core_interaction.lose_state: Player takes too long (over 10 seconds) and their own castle health reaches 0%.

end_card.headline: Epic Comeback!
end_card.visual_treatment: A dynamic freeze-frame of the final explosion with the CTA button overlay.

predicted_appeal.rationale: Creates urgency and shows both core mechanics, but requires more user steps which can drop completion rates.

playable_candidates.implementation_notes_for_codegen: Requires managing two distinct interaction states (UI drag-and-drop vs. in-world slingshot aiming) and a timer for the lose condition.

recommendation_rationale: Candidate A focuses entirely on the most tactile and rewarding mechanic: aiming and shooting. Slingshot mechanics have a proven track record in playable ads due to their intuitive nature and immediate visual feedback.

critical.name: Player Castle
critical.description_for_image_gen: A 2D cartoon medieval stone castle tower with blue roofs, sitting on top of brown tank treads. Side view, thick black outlines, flat colors, transparent background.

critical.name: Enemy Castle
critical.description_for_image_gen: A 2D cartoon medieval stone castle tower with red roofs, sitting on top of brown tank treads. Side view, thick black outlines, flat colors, transparent background.

critical.name: Skeleton Unit
critical.description_for_image_gen: A cute 2D cartoon skeleton wearing a blue helmet and holding a sci-fi blaster gun. Side view facing right, thick outlines, transparent background.

critical.name: Rocket Projectile
critical.description_for_image_gen: A small 2D cartoon red and grey missile/rocket. Side view pointing right, thick outlines, transparent background.

critical.name: Background
critical.description_for_image_gen: A 2D cartoon landscape with rolling green hills, a few stylized trees, and a pale green cloudy sky. Flat colors, simple vector style.

nice_to_have.name: Explosion Particle
nice_to_have.description_for_image_gen: A 2D cartoon explosion cloud, orange and yellow with thick black outlines.
nice_to_have.fallback_if_missing: canvas drawn orange circles expanding and fading

nice_to_have.name: Goblin Unit
nice_to_have.description_for_image_gen: A cute 2D cartoon green goblin wearing a red bandana holding a bazooka.

technical_constraints.framework_rationale: Vanilla Canvas 2D is lightweight enough to easily stay under the 5MB limit while perfectly capable of handling simple 2D physics, trajectory drawing, and sprite rendering without the overhead of a full engine.
known_risks: Calculating the trajectory arc to accurately predict where the projectile will land.
Creating a satisfying visual destruction effect for the enemy castle without using heavy image sequences.

must_pass: Dragging on the player unit renders a visible dotted line indicating trajectory.
Releasing the drag spawns a projectile that follows the indicated trajectory.
Projectile intersecting with the enemy castle bounding box triggers an explosion effect.
Enemy castle health bar decreases upon projectile impact.
After 2 successful hits, the end card UI becomes visible.
should_pass: The trajectory line updates smoothly in real-time as the user drags.
The visual style matches the flat, thick-outlined cartoon aesthetic of the source.
A tutorial hand animation plays if the user does not interact within the first 3 seconds.

things_unobservable_from_videos: Audio and sound effects (video is muted)
What happens if the player's castle reaches 0% health (lose state animation)
